questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 50°. The external angle at B is 120°. Find the measure of angle C in degrees.
questions.explanation: The external angle at B equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles: 120° = A + C = 50° + C. So C = 70°.

questions.question: Two lines intersect inside a triangle, creating four angles at the intersection point. If two opposite angles measure 3x and 2x + 20, find x.
questions.explanation: Opposite angles at an intersection are equal: 3x = 2x + 20. Solving: x = 20.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, the angle bisector from Q meets side PR at point S. If angle PQS = 35° and angle QSR = 110°, find angle QPR in degrees.
questions.explanation: Since QS bisects angle Q, angle PQR = 2(35°) = 70°. In triangle QSR: angle QRS = 180° - 110° - 35° = 35°. In triangle PQR: angle QPR = 180° - 70° - 35° = 55°.

questions.question: A triangle has angles in the ratio 2:3:4. The smallest angle measures how many degrees?
questions.explanation: Let angles be 2k, 3k, 4k. Then 2k + 3k + 4k = 180°, so 9k = 180° and k = 20°. The smallest angle is 2(20°) = 40°.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 60° and angle B = 75°. An altitude from C meets AB at point H. Find angle ACH in degrees.
questions.explanation: Angle C = 180° - 60° - 75° = 45°. In right triangle AHC: angle CAH = 60°, so angle ACH = 90° - 60° = 30°. Wait, recalculate: angle AHC = 90°, angle HAC = 60°, so angle ACH = 30°. Actually angle ACH = 180° - 90° - 60° = 30°... Let me verify: In right triangle CHB: angle CBH = 75°, angle CHB = 90°, so angle HCB = 15°. Since angle ACB = 45°, we have angle ACH = 45° - 15° = 30°. Correction: angle ACH = 15°.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One interior angle on one side of the transversal measures 3x - 10, and the consecutive interior angle measures x + 50. Find x.
questions.explanation: Consecutive interior angles between parallel lines are supplementary: (3x - 10) + (x + 50) = 180°. Simplifying: 4x + 40 = 180°, so 4x = 140° and x = 35.
